results.phrase: We need to edit this look. Not everything makes it to the final face.
results.usage: Use this when a team needs to make cuts but is stalling because no one wants to be the one to remove their feature.
results.topic: we have to cut features before launch but we can't agree on which ones to cut

results.phrase: We're doing a full face in ten minutes. Everyone knows their job.
results.usage: Use this when the team needs to stop deliberating and move into fast, coordinated execution under real time pressure.
results.topic: we are three sprints behind and the big stakeholder review is tomorrow morning

results.phrase: We've been standing at the mirror too long. At some point you just have to set it and go.
results.usage: Use this when a team has been deliberating past the point of usefulness and needs someone to call time on the discussion.
results.topic: we've been debating the same decision for three weeks and nothing is moving

results.phrase: You can't go out with your liner not blended. We need another day.
results.usage: Use this when a launch deadline is being held to even though the product visibly isn't ready.
results.topic: we're supposed to launch next week but the product still has too many rough edges

results.phrase: We keep changing the part mid-blow-dry. Everyone ends up with a different result every time.
results.usage: Use this when shifting direction mid-execution is causing inconsistent outcomes and team frustration.
results.topic: leadership keeps changing the priorities mid-sprint and the team is frustrated
